mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer income, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where the unemployment rate accelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 65% of months where industrial production increases, or the signal shows zero predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days due to earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in > 50% of confirmed recession quarters, or the signal fails to predict direction during documented NBER recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a declining federal funds rate and stable consumer price inflation compresses real interest rates, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and rising aggregate demand, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth months yield negative forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the rank correlation between payroll changes and 20-day returns falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 600,000 units signals a severe housing market contraction that drags down construction spending and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 50% of months where housing starts remain below 600,000, or the signal loses predictive accuracy outside the 2007-2009 crisis window.
